rqs: What are the observed time and space complexities of QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort, Radix Sort, and TimSort versus Insertion, Selection, Bubble, Shell, and Counting Sort in the literature?

Which sorting algorithm among QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort, Radix Sort, and TimSort provides the best stability and lowest number of swaps compared to classic algorithms in studies with data engineers and CS students?
In which scenarios do QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort, Radix Sort, or TimSort outperform Insertion, Selection, Bubble, Shell, or Counting Sort in terms of comparisons and swaps for embedded systems?
base_query: ("researcher") AND ("QuickSort" OR "MergeSort" OR "HeapSort" OR "Radix Sort" OR "TimSort") AND ("Insertion Sort" OR "Selection Sort" OR "Bubble Sort" OR "Shell Sort" OR "Counting Sort") AND ("Time Complexity")
arxiv_query: (all:"researcher") AND (all:"QuickSort" OR all:"MergeSort" OR all:"HeapSort" OR all:"Radix Sort" OR all:"TimSort") AND (all:"Insertion Sort" OR all:"Selection Sort" OR all:"Bubble Sort" OR all:"Shell Sort" OR all:"Counting Sort") AND (all:"Time Complexity")

questions.text: Does the study clearly describe the algorithm implementations and parameters?
Are experimental setups (hardware, datasets, metrics) fully reported?
Is the evaluation methodology (e.g., repeated runs, statistical analysis) appropriate?
Are the results compared against baseline sorting algorithms listed in the PICOC?
Does the paper discuss limitations and applicability of the findings?
